content:
Cow Ghee vs Buffalo Ghee: Which One Should You Buy?

It is the most common question we get asked at NavPrana: “Should I buy cow ghee or buffalo ghee?” The honest answer is — both are excellent, but they are made for different needs. By the end of this guide, you will know exactly which one belongs in your kitchen (and it might even be both).

The Quick Answer

Choose cow ghee if you want a light, easy-to-digest ghee for daily use, Ayurvedic routines, and everyday cooking. Choose buffalo ghee if you want a richer, more nutrient-dense ghee for growing children, active lifestyles, traditional sweets, and better sleep.

Side-by-Side Comparison

FactorDesi Cow A2 GheeBuffalo A2 Ghee
ColourGolden yellow (natural carotene)Creamy white
DigestibilityLighter — ideal for daily useDenser — more filling
Fat & energyModerateHigher — more energy per spoon
Key nutrientsCLA, vitamins A, D, E, KMore butyric acid, calcium & phosphorus
Ayurveda“Laghu” (light), sattvik, balances all three doshasCooling, grounding, promotes strength and sleep
Best forDaily rotis, morning rituals, seniors, weight managementKids, athletes, sweets, winter nourishment, deep sleep
TasteNutty, delicateRich, creamy, indulgent

When Cow Ghee Is the Right Choice

Ayurveda calls cow ghee a sattvik food — light, easy on the gut, and suitable for every dosha in moderation. Pick our Desi Cow A2 Bilona Ghee if:

  • You want ghee for everyday cooking — dal tadka, rotis, sabzi.
  • You follow a morning ritual of ghee in warm water on an empty stomach.
  • You or your parents want a ghee that is gentle on digestion.
  • You are watching your weight — cow ghee's CLA supports fat metabolism.

When Buffalo Ghee Is the Right Choice

Buffalo ghee is the unsung hero of Indian kitchens — richer in butyric acid, calcium, and phosphorus. Pick our Buffalo A2 Bilona Ghee if:

  • You have growing children who need dense, wholesome calories.
  • Your family loves traditional sweets — halwa, laddoo, and puran poli taste noticeably richer.
  • You do physical work or training and need sustained energy.
  • You struggle with sleep — a spoon of buffalo ghee in warm milk at night is a time-tested Ayurvedic remedy.

What Matters More Than Cow vs Buffalo: The Bilona Method

Here is the truth most brands will not tell you: how the ghee is made matters more than which milk it comes from. Factory ghee is extracted directly from cream using machines and high heat, stripping away aroma and nutrients.

At NavPrana, both our cow and buffalo ghee are made the slow way — the traditional Bilona method: whole A2 milk is set into curd, hand-churned into butter, and gently simmered on a low flame. This preserves the butyric acid, fat-soluble vitamins, and the grainy danedar texture that is the signature of genuine ghee.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I use both cow and buffalo ghee together?

Absolutely — many of our customers keep both: cow ghee for daily cooking and morning rituals, buffalo ghee for sweets, kids, and winter months. There is no harm in using both in moderation (1–2 teaspoons a day in total).

Which ghee is better for weight loss?

Cow ghee, taken in moderation. Its CLA content supports fat metabolism, and its lighter profile suits calorie-conscious diets. Remember: quantity matters more than type.

Which ghee is better for babies and children?

Both work, but buffalo ghee's higher calorie density helps healthy weight gain in growing children. For babies starting solids, begin with a few drops of either ghee in dal or khichdi — read our complete guide on ghee for babies.

Why is bilona ghee more expensive than supermarket ghee?

It takes 25–30 litres of A2 milk and hours of hand-churning to make 1 litre of bilona ghee. Factory ghee is mass-extracted from cream in minutes. You are paying for nutrition, not marketing.

The Bottom Line

There is no loser in this comparison — only the wrong ghee for the wrong purpose. Match the ghee to your family's needs:

  • Daily use & light digestion → Desi Cow A2 Bilona Ghee (1 Ltr)
  • Kids, sweets & strength → Buffalo A2 Bilona Ghee (1 Ltr)

Every jar of NavPrana ghee is FSSAI certified, made from grass-fed A2 milk in the Chambal valley, and shipped free on orders above ₹999. Shop the collection →
